untranslatable_reason: Signal requires non-price inputs: (1) IPO date tracking and days-post-IPO calculation (not available in price/technical indicators), (2) ticker symbol phonetic simplicity scoring (linguistic/external data), (3) company name fluency metrics (external/NLP data). These fundamental screening criteria cannot be expressed using only the allowed technical indicators (price, moving averages, RSI, ATR, Bollinger, MACD, ADX, Stochastic, Donchian, Pivots, volume ratios, returns). A backtest engine would need custom data enrichment or external factor integration.
translation_notes: The signal is fundamentally dependent on linguistic/categorical features (pronounceability, company age as IPO, fluency metrics) that exist outside the technical indicator universe. The time-limited holding period (7 days post-IPO) and profit target (+1.5%) can be translated as shown, but entry conditions cannot be properly expressed without IPO metadata and linguistic scoring functions. Recommend implementing as a pre-filter (universe selection) before applying price-based rules.
